reviews.review_text: I get a nostalgic feeling playing this game even though I'm new to the Borderlands series. Really makes you feel like you're part of their world.
Fun to play and the story is entertaining. First looter shooter I've played. It builds the excitement as you look for your next cool item.
good gunplay but story is pretty short and end game is very boring

Honestly, after BL3 I didn't really have hope for the series, but they learned most of their lessons with this game. It is considerably shorter than the previous games, but it is a spinoff. This is also the first game in the series that you can build your character from the ground up, and the character creator does not disappoint in the slightest. Even the classes are all really cool with some very interesting synergies. I love melee, so by the endgame I was a Brrr-zerker and a Stabbomancer. With the new melee weapons you can use, you can really make an insanely strong melee build. Dare I say, they finally created a melee experience more enjoyable than Krieg.

The gunplay is very similar to 3, but with a great fantasy twist. The shotguns were my favorite, especially the ones that shoot a a horizontal blade that is elemental. With my build, the frost shotguns were the best. That was until I found a Casual Frost Swordsplosion at max level, which carried me through the chaos chamber and the rest of the endgame. I swear that thing is a BEAST in this game, and dare I say my favorite shotgun in the series.

The story was alright, and I really think Borderlands' new style of writing thrives best when it doesn't take itself to seriously. They learned the most from 3 in this aspect, and just because this game doesn't have Lilith, it is automatically better than 3. If I have to hear "hey, killer" one more ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ time. Also, yes there's a trans paladin that uses Lorelei's VA from 3. The he/she with a pee/shee barely talks after the first 3 missions and isn't insufferable, so I could care less. However, I did laugh when Tina said she made this character based on someone she met at a coffee shop. Considering that demographic's career prospects, I find this obscenely fitting.

The endgame is just as bad, if not worse than 3 though. The game really could have used some story DLC or proper raid bosses to help with the end game content, but instead we just got wave based dungeons with the same objectives over and over again. The combat is VERY good in this game, but when you're repeating the same combat encounters it can get old fast. I would have loved to see more of Mister Torgue in this game, or better yet, a whole DLC catered to blowing stuff up with him, similar to BL2's Campaign of Carnage DLC. It would have been some really fun worldbuilding outside of the base game too.

Overall, I really do recommend this game, especially if you have a friend to enjoy it with. My buddy and I spent a good amount of time making each other laugh with the character creator, and there are items that work very well with friends. Definitely get it on sale though.

♥♥♥♥ you, Randall S. Pitchford III. Just because you made another game I enjoyed does not let you off the hook.
Good game but doesn't have good replayability it's D&D but Borderlands, and ran by Tina, so yeah it's good

This game is a really refreshing take on the Borderlands series. I don't know more than a handful of things about Dungeons and Dragons, but I really loved the boardgame gimmick. Combine that with the Borderlands art style/humor, along with Tina's fun character, and I'm fully engaged in the premise.

I only played solo campaign and didn't get into the Chaos Chamber stuff btw.
Fun short game, some characters are annoying but overall i had a good time.

If you enjoyed the other borderlands game then you will enjoy this one too. Its in a fantasy DnD setting with decent gun play and story. I enjoyed my time playing and some parts truly had me laughing out loud. Worth a buy when its on sale for sure.
A neat twist on the Borderlands formula that was largely enjoyable. Some random bugs. Worth playing for fans of the series, especially with buddies.

Ask yourself, have you ever wished that Borderlands would reuse the same 4 mob arenas over and over again while your lategame is reduced to spamming "chaos trials" in the main hub, which means you'll be doing random encounters with random mobs in the same places and a loot table so massive there's no point in hoping for anything you've even heard of? Tiny Tina's is for you! Rather than giving thanks to D&D, we'll spend the whole campaign taking every opportunity to laugh at things that WOULD have been interesting, if included to give to the D&D theme, like companions or crafting.

The game even says "roll for initiative" in dialogue once despite having no actual references to rolling for initiative before a battle, even though that WOULD have been a cool way to get those random affixes they're so obsessed with from the Chaos mode.

All in all, they want your money and didn't want to try. You'll spend 20 hours listening to the main villain have a better motivation than old ones like Handsome Jack, yet in classic Borderlands style you aren't ALLOWED to roleplay, despite the entire premise of the game BEING D&D, THE premier game for roleplaying and making choices. Your smug, not customized "Fatemaker" will just crack jokes the whole time as the big bad has a VERY REASONABLE existential crisis that (spoiler) is never explored.

Maybe I'm expecting too much from a Borderlands game, but as I mentioned before, they even broke the LOOT. Depending on what you want, you don't even get the pleasure of the previous games of farming a boss repeatedly for your drop. It may drop in Chaos, which means you need to push FIFTY ranks to even have a chance of a Ascended (BiS) drop, Then, you fight the MASSIVE loot table. Good luck.

This is also getting long, but the stats are a MESS. You get Strength, which gives... crit damage, not damage. You get Dexterity, which gives... MUTIPLICATIVE crit. Get 10 points of Dex (10 levels) for 20% crit so that your gun crit can go from the base 5% to 6%. Yea... don't bother.

The only thing I can say about this game that is nice is that the class I played (wildfire, Mushroom and Wyvern) was interesting when looking at the rest of the series. But when it comes down to it, I beat the final boss, did maybe 2-3 rounds of Chaos, and sat there in front of the NPC wondering if there actually was any loot TO BE HAD.

If it's all random, all pretend, all rotating chambers of idiots to kill, does anything you pick up matter? Go play Doom: Eternal and you don't have to BOTHER reading things and looting stuff anymore, and other than that they have the SAME ENDGAME. Just let that sink in.

This game did not get a lot of love at the time and I get that but I truly enjoyed making my own characters and the gameplay loop was fun, side quests with the standard parodies were amazing, I also met a very kind player who gave me loot to start me off strong! I have loved playing with others. The side quests are funny and thought provoking at times, I loved looking into other forums to see what others asked about building a character. The way the plot ended was amazing
I don't often play games fully but I was fully sucked into this one until I was able to get through so much of the side quests and the full plot.
Fantasy borderlands. If you have played a borderlands game, you know what you're getting.
unironically the best borderlands in the series, coming from a guy who grew up playing thousands of hours of 1 and 2 on the xbox 360.

Seems like it should be good, basically Tiny Tina with Borderlands 3 gameplay. While the RPG elements and character creator seem great. What really kills the game for me are the characters themselves and the writing is so bad. It lacks the vibe of Borderlands 1 and 2.

Fun concept, really liked the character creator. Wish the other games had it instead of pre-determined characters. It's still good old Borderlands. The only thing I don't like so far is the encounter system, it doesn't vibe well.

Bruh, this ain't it. Feels like a bad fever dream, even compared to Borderlands 3.
Gunplay could be great as in BL3, if only there were decent weapons to use. Humor seems over the top in a bad way.
Seem to ran pretty decent at least in the early parts of the game.
2K Terms of Service are also a topic of their own. Would not recommend.
It's a fun borderlands offshoot. But I am getting a bit tired of the series as it is very repetitive.  8/10

its a good game. the gun play, the guns, the classes all good.  the art is top notch. the dialog is good.  the worst parts are tiny tina is not the tiny tina from the main games but i get over that by thinking of her as  an alternate dimension tiny tina sorta like rick and morty. valintine and the robot are wierd they are players without characters makeing active decisions in the game but without characters there is no concequence to them. their comentary is fun but i would like an in game reason why they have input like a player but no representation in game other than commentary.  the perfect voices of the npc characters is a bit wierd since it doesent represent the character co gming for a short time to me  an imperfect impression of the voice in tiny tinas voice would have been more in line. but all in all its more good than bad and the bad is just minor quibbles on my part.
Super fun first person shooter. The ongoing narration by TIny Tina is hilarious.

Suprisingly good execution of the fantasy take on the borderlands formula. Whilst I didn't get as invested into the characaters as in Assault on Dragon Keep the story was still engaging enough to play through multiple times.

I have hundreds of games and this is possibly the worst multiplayer game I have ever played. After finishing a great borderlands 3 run with my friends we decided to buy this overpriced price of absolute junk for 60 dollars on release and not only does the multiplayer suck but the entire story went from boring and dumb to cringe and mentally disabled. Heavily do not recommend.

Tiny Tina's Wonderlands hat eine langweilige Story, die deutlich schlechter als Borderlands 2 ist. Gerettet wird sie nur durch Witz und Absurdität. 
Das Levelscaling lässt jeden Stufenaufstieg wie eine Bestrafung anfühlen. Viele Gebiete werden dadurch mit der Zeit im Vergleich zum aufgelevelten Charakter schwerer. Klar kann das mit dem wachsenden Skill ausgeglichen werden, aber man hat kein Gefühl von Progression. Ein Level up heißt nur, dass alle Waffen und Gegenstände im Vergleich zu den Gegnern schlechter werden und dass man sich früher oder später um Ersatz bemühen muss. 
Viele Kämpfe sind Zufallsbegegnungen auf den immer gleichen Karten / in den gleichen Arenen. Hat man genügend getötet, verschwinden alle Gegener auf magische Weise. Extremst repetetiv.
Dazu viel zu viel nutzloser Loot. Man wirft relativ schnell alles, was nicht selten ist, ohne genaueres Hinschauen weg. Fühlt sich an wie ein Inventarmanagerspiel. Das war in Borderlands 2 auch deutlich besser.
Andy Samberg constantly saying stupid ♥♥♥♥ made me uninstall before 2 hours
The amount of Anita Sarkeesian slop force fed down your throat in this game is appalling. Ciaran Strange is a god awful voice actress and just needs to stop.
best borderlands game
I always wondered where the numbers go... NUMBERS! GO! UP!
gust not super fun
it is so good
